search_queries_used: site:dha.gov.ae dysmenorrhoea telehealth clinical guideline PDF
rejection_reasons: Issue 2 is the current official DHA copy.
Patient information was not treated as clinician workflow guidance.
population_applicability: Partial: the exact source is adult-focused and the broad workflow does not define age or exclude pregnancy-related and secondary causes.
setting_applicability: Partial: the exact source is Dubai telehealth; the baseline is setting-agnostic and includes in-person examination fields.
UAE_applicability: Verified for DHA-licensed telehealth in Dubai, United Arab Emirates; broader UAE application is not asserted.
recency_verification: Official Issue 2 copy dated 2024-02-21, effective 2024-04-21, revision due 2029-02-21, opened on 2026-07-12.
superseded_check: Issue 2 is the current document linked by DHA; older material was rejected.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Directly supports mapped dysmenorrhoea terminology, menstrual and pain history, associated symptoms, functional impact, and prior response fields in the stated population.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Defines the Dubai telehealth setting and menstrual, pain, associated symptom, functional impact, contraception, medical, surgical, and sexual history required for dysmenorrhoea assessment.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Directly supports mapped concern prompts and specific negatives only when assessed or reported.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Lists sudden or constant severe pain, peritonism, fever, abnormal bleeding or discharge, instability, anaemia, weight loss, infertility, dyspareunia, non-midline pain, and progressive symptoms as concern features.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped lifestyle and clinician-documented follow-up discussion without importing medication doses.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Describes lifestyle measures and follow-up after teleconsultation; medication doses were reviewed but are not mapped into the workflow.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped warning, worsening, and clinician-arranged review documentation.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Defines emergency and specialist referral contexts for sudden or severe pain, peritonism, pregnancy concern, abnormal bleeding, fever or discharge, progressive symptoms, and suspected secondary pathology.

unresolved_source_gaps: Adolescent-specific dysmenorrhoea, face-to-face pelvic examination, pelvic-mass symptoms, urinary symptoms, medication selection, medication doses, and fixed legacy follow-up intervals remain unsupported.
The primary-dysmenorrhoea label must remain clinician-stated rather than inferred.
Qualified clinician review has not been completed.
